summary: A fair‑quality audience tape that still delivers a scorching show – Help>Slip>Franklin’s, The Other One and Days Between shine.
review: Even though the recording is not a crisp soundboard (some sections are mono or thin), the performance on this night is consistently praised. Highlights include a smoking Help → Slip → Franklin’s Tower, an incendiary The Other One and one of the best Days Between ever captured, while Wave To The Wind drags down a few moments. Overall the music outweighs the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rave about the musical set (show quality ~4.5) but note inconsistent or sub‑par recording quality (~2.5). Weighted average yields a solid 3.9.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Not a true SBD; many describe it as the best‑sounding AUD with sections that are thin, flimsy or mono up through Music Never Stopped. Later tracks (e.g., Aiko) sound excellent.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid, but the second set really erupts with blues‑y Rooster, a tight Help>Slip>Franklin’s, a fiery Other One and an emotional Days Between. The encore Liberty caps a high‑energy night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as smiling and in a good mood; uses MIDI on The Other One; guitar work generally praised though one reviewer found the show unfocused.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s piano shines on Franklin's Tower and adds sparkle throughout.
